maml:description.maml:para: Add GPO links to AD containers
This cmdlet will let you link an existing GPO to a container in AD, specifying the position of the link.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the AD container path that you wish to link to (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)

Provide the numeric position of the link within the container. To place the link at the bottom of the list, use -1. To place it at the top of the list, use 1. To place it 3rd in the list, 3, etc. If you specify a value that is greater than the number of existing links on the container, an error will result.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the AD container path that you wish to link to (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)

Provide the numeric position of the link within the container. To place the link at the bottom of the list, use -1. To place it at the top of the list, use 1. To place it 3rd in the list, 3, etc. If you specify a value that is greater than the number of existing links on the container, an error will result.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the AD container path that you wish to link to (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)

Provide the numeric position of the link within the container. To place the link at the bottom of the list, use -1. To place it at the top of the list, use 1. To place it 3rd in the list, 3, etc. If you specify a value that is greater than the number of existing links on the container, an error will result.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example links the "My GPO" GPO to the Marketing OU in the last position (i.e. lowest precedence).

Add-SDMgplink -name "my GPO" -Scope "OU=Sales,DC=cpandl,DC=com" -Location -1
GPOInheritanceBlock Name Path Type ed ------------------- ---- ---- ---- False Marketing OU=Marketing,DC=cpan... somOU

command:example.maml:title: -------------- Example 2 --------------
command:example.dev:code: add-sdmgplink -Name "My GPO" -Scope "OU=Marketing, DC= child, DC=cpandl, DC=com" -Location -1 -DomainName "child.cpandl.com"

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example shows the use of the DomainName parameter to link the "My GPO" GPO in domain child.cpandl.com to the Marketing OU in the last position (i.e. lowest precedence).

maml:description.maml:para: Add security permission to a GPO
Lets you add one of 4 pre-defined permission types to a GPO for a particular AD security principal.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to grant to a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Apply Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to process a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Edit Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to edit a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Modify permission that allows the trustee to edit, modify security and delete a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Read Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to read a GPO only (not apply). NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to grant to a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Apply Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to process a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Edit Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to edit a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Modify permission that allows the trustee to edit, modify security and delete a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Read Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to read a GPO only (not apply). NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to grant to a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Apply Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to process a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to grant to a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Edit Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to edit a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to grant to a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Modify permission that allows the trustee to edit, modify security and delete a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to grant to a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Read Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to read a GPO only (not apply). NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to grant to a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Apply Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to process a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Edit Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to edit a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Modify permission that allows the trustee to edit, modify security and delete a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Specifies the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Read Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to read a GPO only (not apply). NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Add GP Editors group with editing rights to the "Folder Redirection Policy" GPO
add-sdmgposecurity -Name "Folder Redirection Policy" -Trustee "cpandl\GP Editors" -PermEdit

Inherited : False Inheritable : True Denied : False Permission : 65536 Trustee : System.__ComObject
Inherited : False Inheritable : True Denied : False Permission : 65794 Trustee : System.__ComObject
Inherited : False Inheritable : True Denied : False Permission : 65794 Trustee : System.__ComObject

maml:description.maml:para: Add GP-related security permission to a Scope of Management (SOM).
This cmdlet modifies the ACL on SOM objects (e.g. Sites, Domains and OUs) to allow GP-specific permissions to be applied to trustees (i.e. users and groups).
Provide the AD container path that you wish to add GP related permissions on (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)
Provide the user or group name that you wish to grant to a SOM. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

Specifies the permission that grants the user or group the Create GPO permission that allows the trustee to create a GPO in the domain. If you use this option, you must specify the domain DN on the Scope parameter NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

Specifies the permission that grants the user or group the Link GPO permission that allows the trustee to Link a GPO to a site, domain or OU. NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

Specifies the permission that grants the user or group the Generate RSOP Logging permission that allows the trustee to run RSOP Logging (Group Policy Results) against targets in the Scope specified. NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

Specifies the permission that grants the user or group the Generate RSOP Planning permission that allows the trustee to run RSOP Planning (Group Policy Modeling) against targets in the Scope specified. NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.
Provide the AD container path that you wish to add GP related permissions on (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)
Provide the user or group name that you wish to grant to a SOM. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

Specifies the permission that grants the user or group the Create GPO permission that allows the trustee to create a GPO in the domain. If you use this option, you must specify the domain DN on the Scope parameter NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

Specifies the permission that grants the user or group the Link GPO permission that allows the trustee to Link a GPO to a site, domain or OU. NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

Specifies the permission that grants the user or group the Generate RSOP Logging permission that allows the trustee to run RSOP Logging (Group Policy Results) against targets in the Scope specified. NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

Specifies the permission that grants the user or group the Generate RSOP Planning permission that allows the trustee to run RSOP Planning (Group Policy Modeling) against targets in the Scope specified. NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

dev:remarks.maml:para: In this example, we are adding the Create GPO permission for the group called GPO Admins, to the cpandl.com domain.

maml:description.maml:para: Adds a WMI Filter to a particular GPO
Use this cmdlet to link an existing WMI filter to a specified GPO
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the name of the WMI filter to link to the GPO.
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the name of the WMI filter to link to the GPO.
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the name of the WMI filter to link to the GPO.
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Links the WMI filter called "Laptop Test" to the GPO called "Wireless Policy"
Filter Laptop test linked to GPO Wireless Policy

maml:description.maml:para: Copies an existing Starter GPO to a new one.
With this cmdlet, you can copy an existing Starter GPO, including its settings, to a new Starter GPO in the same domain.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the friendly name of the new, destination Starter GPO that will be created.
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the friendly name of the new, destination Starter GPO that will be created.
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

maml:alert.maml:para: Note that this cmdlet only works on Windows Server 2008, Vista, SP1 and above

command:example.maml:title: -------------- Example 1 --------------
command:example.dev:code: Copy-SDMStarterGPO "IE Security Starter GPO" -NewStarterName "Test IE Security Starter"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Copies an existing Starter GPO called "IE Security Starter", to a new Starter GPO with the name "Test IE Security Starter".

DisplayName : Test IE Security Starter Description : This Starter GPO contains organization-wide settings for Internet Explorer security zone configurat ions Author : Product : CreationTime : 6/25/2008 6:39:56 PM ID : {E2B67F3E-180F-488F-8931-8CA41D3825FC} ModifiedTime : 6/25/2008 6:39:56 PM Type : 1 ComputerVersion : 1 UserVersion : 1 StarterGPOVersion :

maml:description.maml:para: Backup a GPO by Name
Performs a GPMC backup of a GPO that you specify by friendly name, to the backup folder path you specify
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Path to the backup folder. Can be either a literal path (e.g. c:\gpbackups) or a UNC path (\\server1\gpbackups)
Provide a comment that is stored with the backup. The comment is optional but can be any descriptive string.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Path to the backup folder. Can be either a literal path (e.g. c:\gpbackups) or a UNC path (\\server1\gpbackups)
Provide a comment that is stored with the backup. The comment is optional but can be any descriptive string.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Path to the backup folder. Can be either a literal path (e.g. c:\gpbackups) or a UNC path (\\server1\gpbackups)
Provide a comment that is stored with the backup. The comment is optional but can be any descriptive string.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

maml:alert.maml:para: Cmdlet will return a "Backup Successful" message on success.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Backs up a GPO called "2008 GPO" to the path \\sdm1\c$\gpbackups, with a comment of "Test Backup"

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves a list of linked GPOs on specified AD container

Given an AD scope which is a DN to a site, domain or OU, this cmdlet returns a list of linked GPOs by GUID. You can also use this cmdlet to modify the properties of a link, such as the Enabled and Enforced properties, by setting them to $true or $false
Provide the AD container path that you wish to return a list of linked GPOs on (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
If this parameter is specified, the native GPMGPOLink COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMGPOLink is returned instead.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
If this parameter is specified, the native GPMGPOLink COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMGPOLink is returned instead.
Provide the AD container path that you wish to return a list of linked GPOs on (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
If this parameter is specified, the native GPMGPOLink COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMGPOLink is returned instead.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example retrieves a list of GPO links for the Marketing OU.

GPOID : {3C41D4AE-CCDF-4640-998D-D9A24EC48086} GPODomain : cpandl.com Enabled : True Enforced : False SOMLinkOrder : 1 SOM : GPMGMTLib.GPMSOMClass
GPOID : {95C79242-CE65-4BF8-AC87-7678D2A85EA3} GPODomain : cpandl.com Enabled : False Enforced : False SOMLinkOrder : 2 SOM : GPMGMTLib.GPMSOMClass
GPOID : {A9E790E5-9963-40F9-A479-BD68DEDD921C} GPODomain : cpandl.com Enabled : False Enforced : False SOMLinkOrder : 3 SOM : GPMGMTLib.GPMSOMClass
GPOID : {0AA65012-F87F-4281-988A-62081B9F3686} GPODomain : cpandl.com Enabled : True Enforced : False SOMLinkOrder : 4 SOM : GPMGMTLib.GPMSOMClass
Searches for all GP Links associated with the GPO called "Container Test"
GPOInheritanceBlocked Name Path Type --------------------- ---- ---- ---- False Vista Test OU=Vista Test,DC=cpandl,DC... 2 False Users OU=Users,OU=Vista Test,DC=... 2

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves information about one or all GPOs in a domain

This cmdlet returns detailed information about GPOs in a domain. You can specify a particular GPO by name or all GPOs, and what is returned is an object that contains properties about each GPO, such as its GUID, its version, its path in AD, etc.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

If this parameter is specified, the native GPMGPO COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMGPO is returned instead. Note that the COM interop type does not work well with the PowerShell pipeline. Hence, if you want to leverage the pipeline to output the contents of this cmdlet, you should not specify -Native.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

If this parameter is specified, the native GPMGPO COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMGPO is returned instead. Note that the COM interop type does not work well with the PowerShell pipeline. Hence, if you want to leverage the pipeline to output the contents of this cmdlet, you should not specify -Native.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

If this parameter is specified, the native GPMGPO COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMGPO is returned instead. Note that the COM interop type does not work well with the PowerShell pipeline. Hence, if you want to leverage the pipeline to output the contents of this cmdlet, you should not specify -Native.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves information about the Default Domain Policy GPO
get-sdmGPO "Default Domain Policy"

DisplayName : Default Domain Policy Path : cn={31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9},cn=poli cies,cn=system,DC=cpandl,DC=com ID : {31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9} DomainName : cpandl.com CreationTime : 11/11/2004 4:20:24 PM ModificationTime : 9/25/2007 12:06:20 PM UserDSVersionNumber : 9 ComputerDSVersionNumber : 33 UserSysvolVersionNumber : 9 ComputerSysvolVersionNumber : 33
Searches for a GPO with a GUID of {31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9}.

CreationTime : 11/11/2004 5:20:24 PM Name : Default Domain Policy DomainName : cpandl.com ID : {31b2f340-016d-11d2-945f-00c04fb984f9} ModificationTime : 6/9/2008 2:33:02 PM Path : cn={31b2f340-016d-11d2-945f-00c04fb984f9},cn=policies,cn=system,DC=cpandl,DC=com UserDSVersionNumber : 10 UserSysvolVersionNumber : 10 ComputerDSVersionNumber : 38 ComputerSysvolVersionNumber : 38 Description :

maml:description.maml:para: Returns a list of all GPO backups for a given file location
Backups made using GPMC (or export-sdmgpo) are stored in a file system directory you specify. This cmdlet returns a list of either all backups or backups for a specified GPO name
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Path to the backup folder containing GPMC backups. Can be either a literal path (e.g. c:\gpbackups) or a UNC path (\\server1\gpbackups)
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Path to the backup folder containing GPMC backups. Can be either a literal path (e.g. c:\gpbackups) or a UNC path (\\server1\gpbackups)
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Path to the backup folder containing GPMC backups. Can be either a literal path (e.g. c:\gpbackups) or a UNC path (\\server1\gpbackups)
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all backups for the GPO Default Domain Policy from the folder \\sdm1\gpbackups. Note that the ID property on the returned objects can be used with restore-SDMgpo.

ID : {071C6314-DE45-4456-8F36-30A7B03F0404} GPOID : {31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9} GPODomain : cpandl.com GPODisplayName : Default Domain Policy Timestamp : 11/6/2007 1:18:14 PM Comment : BackupDir : \\sdm1\gpbackups
ID : {0C1F3FF0-A7B0-4CDC-89DB-AD882FF6E811} GPOID : {31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9} GPODomain : cpandl.com GPODisplayName : Default Domain Policy Timestamp : 9/18/2007 9:46:30 AM Comment : foo32 BackupDir : \\sdm1\gpbackups
ID : {40440C9B-8501-46C0-915F-99E79610C454} GPOID : {31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9} GPODomain : west.cpandl.com GPODisplayName : Default Domain Policy Timestamp : 9/18/2007 9:43:38 AM Comment : west333 BackupDir : \\sdm1\gpbackups

maml:description.maml:para: Returns the owner (AD user or group) of the GPO
This cmdlet retrieves and returns a string object that is the owner of the GPO. It is represented as domain\user or group (e.g. cpandl\domain admins).
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns a string that is the name of the user or group currently assigned as the owner of the GPO.
Cpandl\Domain Admins

maml:description.maml:para: Returns a list of Permissions on a GPO
Given a GPO friendly name, this cmdlet returns the list of groups, computers and users that have permissions on the GPO and what those permissions are
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

dev:type.maml:name: System.Collections.Generic.List<GPOPermission>: GPOPermission is a class containing string properties that indicate, Trustee, Permission and (boolean) Inherited flag

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the permissions on the Default Domain Policy GPO

Get-SDMgpoSecurity "Default Domain Policy"
Trustee Permission Inherited ------- ---------- --------- NT AUTHORITY\Authentica... permGPOApply False CPANDL\Domain Admins permGPOEditSecurityAndD... False CPANDL\Version Control ... permGPOEditSecurityAndD... False CPANDL\Enterprise Admins permGPOEditSecurityAndD... False NT AUTHORITY\ENTERPRISE... permGPORead False NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM permGPOEditSecurityAndD... False

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieve GP-related security permission to a Scope of Management (SOM).
This cmdlet retrieves the ACL on SOM objects (e.g. Sites, Domains and OUs) to obtain GP-specific permissions
Provide the AD container path that you wish to retrieve GP related permissions on (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the AD container path that you wish to retrieve GP related permissions on (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example retrieves GP-related permissions from the Marketing OU. These permissions include the right to link a GPO, and rights for RSoP Logging and Planning

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves a Starter GPO by Name
This cmdlet will retrieve a reference to a Starter GPO by name.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

If this parameter is specified, the native GPMTemplate COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMGPMStarterGPO is returned instead. Note that the COM interop type does not work well with the PowerShell pipeline. Hence, if you want to leverage the pipeline to output the contents of this cmdlet, you should not specify -Native.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

If this parameter is specified, the native GPMTemplate COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMGPMStarterGPO is returned instead. Note that the COM interop type does not work well with the PowerShell pipeline. Hence, if you want to leverage the pipeline to output the contents of this cmdlet, you should not specify -Native.

maml:alert.maml:para: Note that this cmdlet works on Windows Server 2008, Vista, SP1 and newer with RSAT

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example retrieves a reference to the Starter GPO called "IE Security Starter GPO"

CreationTime : 9/26/2007 9:53:23 PM Name : IE Security Starter GPO ID : {6CA738F7-C593-4C3A-A8C4-87C0B91AECC7} ModifiedTime : 9/26/2007 9:53:24 PM Product : UserVersion : 0 StarterGPOVersion : ComputerVersion : 0 Author : Description : This Starter GPO contains organization-wide settings for Internet Explorer security zone configurat ions Type : typeCustom

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves a reference to one or all WMI Filters in a domain
This cmdlet retrieves information about WMI Filters within the domain.
Specify the name of the WMI Filter to retrieve or specify * to retrieve all WMI Filters in a domain
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

If this parameter is specified, the native GPMWMIFilter COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMWMIFilter is returned instead. Note that the COM interop type does not work well with the PowerShell pipeline. Hence, if you want to leverage the pipeline to output the contents of this cmdlet, you should not specify -Native.
Specify the name of the WMI Filter to retrieve or specify * to retrieve all WMI Filters in a domain
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

If this parameter is specified, the native GPMWMIFilter COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMWMIFilter is returned instead. Note that the COM interop type does not work well with the PowerShell pipeline. Hence, if you want to leverage the pipeline to output the contents of this cmdlet, you should not specify -Native.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves information about a WMI Filter called "Laptop Test" and pipes it to format-list to be more readable
Description : Name : Laptop test Path : MSFT_SomFilter.Domain="cpandl.com",ID="{4BBBC23D-D082-4E6F-A033-0988289BF4FE}" QueryStrings : {root\CIMv2;Select * from Win32_Battery}

maml:description.maml:para: Performs a GPO import from backup. Useful when importing a GPO from one domain to another.

This cmdlet performs a GPMC import of a GPO backup. Note that you must specify a target GPO that you want to import onto. That target GPO is overwritten when the import is completed. You can also optionally provide a migration table name. Migration tables are used to map security principals and UNC paths when importing a GPO from one domain or forest into another
This is the ID of the Backup you wish to restore. It takes the form of a GUID string. NOTE: This is not the GUID of the GPO but rather the GUID of the GPO Backup.

The location where the GPO backup files reside. Note that this can be a literal path (e.g. c:\gpbackups) or a UNC path (\\server1\gpbackups). This is a folder path, not a path to a specific backup folder.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).

This optional parameters takes the DNS name (e.g. cpandl.com) of the domain that you wish to operate on. You must tell GPMC which domain you wish to operate on if it is not the same domain as where the cmdlet is running. Use this if the GPO you are importing onto exists in a domain different from the one where you are running the cmdlet.

This parameter expects the full path the migration table file (.migtable file) that you wish to use during the import. This can be either a UNC path (e.g. \\server\share\import.migtable) or literal drive path (e.g. c:\migrations\import.migtable). This parameter is optional. If not migration table path is specified, then security principals and UNC paths from the source GPO backup are left as is in the imported GPO.

This switch parameter is only used if you specify a migration table using the MigrationTableLocation parameter. If you select this parameter, then the import process holds to a strict migration. What this means is that if any security principals or UNC paths found in the GPO backup are not accounted for in the migration table you've specified, then the import fails. If you do not specify this parameter but do specify a migration table, then the import process will use the migration table where it can but will leave unmatched principals and paths as-is in the imported GPO.
This is the ID of the Backup you wish to restore. It takes the form of a GUID string. NOTE: This is not the GUID of the GPO but rather the GUID of the GPO Backup.

The location where the GPO backup files reside. Note that this can be a literal path (e.g. c:\gpbackups) or a UNC path (\\server1\gpbackups). This is a folder path, not a path to a specific backup folder.
Provide the GUID of the GPO you wish to import, using the format of "{B3E21B16-9881-425C-AEB3-C009AA8CD065}". Note that you can specify either the Name parameter or the GUID one, but not both.

This optional parameters takes the DNS name (e.g. cpandl.com) of the domain that you wish to operate on. You must tell GPMC which domain you wish to operate on if it is not the same domain as where the cmdlet is running. Use this if the GPO you are importing onto exists in a domain different from the one where you are running the cmdlet.

This parameter expects the full path the migration table file (.migtable file) that you wish to use during the import. This can be either a UNC path (e.g. \\server\share\import.migtable) or literal drive path (e.g. c:\migrations\import.migtable). This parameter is optional. If not migration table path is specified, then security principals and UNC paths from the source GPO backup are left as is in the imported GPO.

This switch parameter is only used if you specify a migration table using the MigrationTableLocation parameter. If you select this parameter, then the import process holds to a strict migration. What this means is that if any security principals or UNC paths found in the GPO backup are not accounted for in the migration table you've specified, then the import fails. If you do not specify this parameter but do specify a migration table, then the import process will use the migration table where it can but will leave unmatched principals and paths as-is in the imported GPO.
This is the ID of the Backup you wish to restore. It takes the form of a GUID string. NOTE: This is not the GUID of the GPO but rather the GUID of the GPO Backup.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).

The location where the GPO backup files reside. Note that this can be a literal path (e.g. c:\gpbackups) or a UNC path (\\server1\gpbackups). This is a folder path, not a path to a specific backup folder.

This optional parameters takes the DNS name (e.g. cpandl.com) of the domain that you wish to operate on. You must tell GPMC which domain you wish to operate on if it is not the same domain as where the cmdlet is running. Use this if the GPO you are importing onto exists in a domain different from the one where you are running the cmdlet.

This parameter expects the full path the migration table file (.migtable file) that you wish to use during the import. This can be either a UNC path (e.g. \\server\share\import.migtable) or literal drive path (e.g. c:\migrations\import.migtable). This parameter is optional. If not migration table path is specified, then security principals and UNC paths from the source GPO backup are left as is in the imported GPO.

This switch parameter is only used if you specify a migration table using the MigrationTableLocation parameter. If you select this parameter, then the import process holds to a strict migration. What this means is that if any security principals or UNC paths found in the GPO backup are not accounted for in the migration table you've specified, then the import fails. If you do not specify this parameter but do specify a migration table, then the import process will use the migration table where it can but will leave unmatched principals and paths as-is in the imported GPO.
Provide the GUID of the GPO you wish to import, using the format of "{B3E21B16-9881-425C-AEB3-C009AA8CD065}". Note that you can specify either the Name parameter or the GUID one, but not both.

dev:remarks.maml:para: In this example, I'm importing a backup with the ID of {CAEF2C15-0F1A-47EF-B3CF-5FBC6D158C7E} into a GPO in my current domain called "My Import GPO". I'm instructing the cmdlet to use a migration table called myimport.migtable and to import it strictly (using the MigrationTableOnly param)

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new GPO in the domain specified
This cmdlet creates a new GPO in the domain specified. The GPO is not linked to anything so you can use the add-sdmgplink cmdlet to link it to a container.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

(Only supported when run from Server 2008 or Vista, SP1 systems). This parameter, when supplied, lets you create a new GPO from an existing Starter GPO. Specify the name of the Starter GPO you wish to create from.

If this parameter is specified, the native GPMGPO COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMGPO is returned instead. Note that the COM interop type does not work well with the PowerShell pipeline. Hence, if you want to leverage the pipeline to output the contents of this cmdlet, you should not specify -Native.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

(Only supported when run from Server 2008 or Vista, SP1 systems). This parameter, when supplied, lets you create a new GPO from an existing Starter GPO. Specify the name of the Starter GPO you wish to create from.

If this parameter is specified, the native GPMGPO COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMGPO is returned instead. Note that the COM interop type does not work well with the PowerShell pipeline. Hence, if you want to leverage the pipeline to output the contents of this cmdlet, you should not specify -Native.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new GPO called Engineering Desktop Lockdown in the cpandl.com AD domain
new-sdmgpo "Engineering Desktop Lockdown" -Domain "cpandl.com"

DisplayName : Engineering Desktop Lockdown Path : cn={3DFEE9B7-7D3A-4805-B6E9-F75418043DCC},cn=poli cies,cn=system,DC=cpandl,DC=com ID : {3DFEE9B7-7D3A-4805-B6E9-F75418043DCC} DomainName : cpandl.com CreationTime : 11/7/2007 5:16:40 PM ModificationTime : 11/7/2007 5:17:14 PM UserDSVersionNumber : 0 ComputerDSVersionNumber : 0 UserSysvolVersionNumber : 0 ComputerSysvolVersionNumber : 0

command:example.maml:title: -------------- Example 2 --------------
command:example.dev:code: new-sdmGPO "New GPO From Starter" -FromStarterGPO "IE Security Starter GPO"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new GPO called "New GPO From Starter", based on the Starter GPO called "IE Security Starter GPO"

CreationTime : 6/26/2008 11:58:28 AM Name : New GPO From Starter DomainName : cpandl.com ID : {CF241AFA-F776-4448-A734-65CF1D3679A9} ModificationTime : 6/26/2008 11:58:28 AM Path : cn={CF241AFA-F776-4448-A734-65CF1D3679A9},cn=policies,cn=system,DC=cpandl,DC=com UserDSVersionNumber : 1 UserSysvolVersionNumber : 1 ComputerDSVersionNumber : 1 ComputerSysvolVersionNumber : 1 Description :

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new Starter GPO in the Domain.
This cmdlet lets you create a new Starter GPO in a given domain.
Provide the Starter GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the Starter GPO you wish to manage.

If this parameter is specified, the native GPMTemplate COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMGPMStarterGPO is returned instead. Note that the COM interop type does not work well with the PowerShell pipeline. Hence, if you want to leverage the pipeline to output the contents of this cmdlet, you should not specify -Native.
Provide the Starter GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the Starter GPO you wish to manage.

If this parameter is specified, the native GPMTemplate COM Interop type is returned to the pipeline. If not, then the custom type SDMGPMStarterGPO is returned instead. Note that the COM interop type does not work well with the PowerShell pipeline. Hence, if you want to leverage the pipeline to output the contents of this cmdlet, you should not specify -Native.

maml:alert.maml:para: Note that this cmdlet only works on Windows Server 2008 and Vista, SP1 with RSAT

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new Starter GPO called "My New Starter" in the domain called west.cpandl.com

CreationTime : 6/26/2008 12:04:00 PM Name : My New Starter ID : {77C9A2D8-1C7C-422D-9E2C-6707657398AB} ModifiedTime : 6/26/2008 12:04:00 PM Product : UserVersion : 0 StarterGPOVersion : ComputerVersion : 0 Author : Description : Type : typeCustom

maml:description.maml:para: Output GPO settings to the pipeline as a collection of objects

This cmdlet provide a way of document GPO settings separate from the standard GPMC XML or HTML settings reports. This output uses SDM Software GP parsing technology to output settings as a collection of GPSettingObject objects, which have properties: domain name, GPO name, Setting Path and Setting Value. The cmdlet allows you to specify specific policy areas you wish to report on as well.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

A comma-separated list of policy areas you wish to output from a given GPO. If this parameter is not included, then all policy areas are included (default). Possible policy areas are case sensitive and come from the following list: 'Registry','Software Installation','Security','LanSvc Networks','Windows Firewall','Scripts','Remote Installation','WLanSvc Networks','Folder Redirection','Software Restriction','IP security','Internet Explorer Maintenance','Policy-based QoS','Deployed Printer Connections Policy',Network Access Protection Client Management','Advanced Audit Configuration','Name Resolution Policy','Public Key','Application Control Policies','Environment Variables','Local Users and Groups','Devices','Network Options','Folders','Network Shares','Files','Data Sources','Ini Files','Services','Folder Options','Scheduled Tasks','Windows Registry','Printers','Shortcuts','Power Options','Drive Maps','Start Menu','Regional Options','Internet Options'
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

A comma-separated list of policy areas you wish to output from a given GPO. If this parameter is not included, then all policy areas are included (default). Possible policy areas are case sensitive and come from the following list: 'Registry','Software Installation','Security','LanSvc Networks','Windows Firewall','Scripts','Remote Installation','WLanSvc Networks','Folder Redirection','Software Restriction','IP security','Internet Explorer Maintenance','Policy-based QoS','Deployed Printer Connections Policy',Network Access Protection Client Management','Advanced Audit Configuration','Name Resolution Policy','Public Key','Application Control Policies','Environment Variables','Local Users and Groups','Devices','Network Options','Folders','Network Shares','Files','Data Sources','Ini Files','Services','Folder Options','Scheduled Tasks','Windows Registry','Printers','Shortcuts','Power Options','Drive Maps','Start Menu','Regional Options','Internet Options'
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

A comma-separated list of policy areas you wish to output from a given GPO. If this parameter is not included, then all policy areas are included (default). Possible policy areas are case sensitive and come from the following list: 'Registry','Software Installation','Security','LanSvc Networks','Windows Firewall','Scripts','Remote Installation','WLanSvc Networks','Folder Redirection','Software Restriction','IP security','Internet Explorer Maintenance','Policy-based QoS','Deployed Printer Connections Policy',Network Access Protection Client Management','Advanced Audit Configuration','Name Resolution Policy','Public Key','Application Control Policies','Environment Variables','Local Users and Groups','Devices','Network Options','Folders','Network Shares','Files','Data Sources','Ini Files','Services','Folder Options','Scheduled Tasks','Windows Registry','Printers','Shortcuts','Power Options','Drive Maps','Start Menu','Regional Options','Internet Options'
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet provides a subset of the GPO reporting and analysis features found in the SDM Software GPO Reporting Pak

command:example.maml:title: EXAMPLE 1

maml:introduction.maml:para: Outputs all settings for GPO "Default Domain Policy" to the pipeline

command:example.dev:code:
C:\PS> Out-SDMGPSettings "Default Domain Policy"
Domain DisplayName SettingPath ------ ----------- ----------- cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Account Policies... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Account Policies... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Account Policies... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Account Policies... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Account Policies... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Account Policies... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Account Policies... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Account Policies... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Account Policies... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Account Policies... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Account Policies... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Account Policies... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Local Policies|S... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Advanced Audit C... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Advanced Audit C... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Settings|Advanced Audit C... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Administrative Templates|System|Display highly deta... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Administrative Templates|System|Group Policy|Allow ... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Administrative Templates|Windows Components|Remote ... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Administrative Templates|Windows Components|Remote ... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Administrative Templates|Windows Components|Remote ... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Administrative Templates|Windows Components|Remote ... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Administrative Templates|Windows Components|Windows... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy Computer Configuration|Policies|Administrative Templates|Windows Components|Windows... cpandl.com Default Domain Policy User Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Remote Installation|Automatic Setup cpandl.com Default Domain Policy User Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Remote Installation|Custom Setup cpandl.com Default Domain Policy User Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Remote Installation|Restart Setup cpandl.com Default Domain Policy User Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Remote Installation|Tools

command:example.maml:title: EXAMPLE 2

maml:introduction.maml:para: Outputs just "Windows Firewall" policy for a GPO named "Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline"

command:example.dev:code:
C:\PS> Out-SDMGPSettings "Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline" -Areas "Windows Firewall"
Domain DisplayName SettingPath ------ ----------- ----------- cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set... cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set... cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set... cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set... cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set... cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set... cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set... cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set... cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set... cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set... cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set... cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set... cpandl.com Server 2012 Member Server Security Baseline Computer Configuration|Policies|Windows Settings|Security Set...

maml:description.maml:para: Outputs a GP Settings report in either HTML or XML format
This cmdlet creates a GPMC GPO Settings report for the specified GPO and outputs it to either the console or a file, in either an XML or HTML format.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
This optional parameter specifies the file path you wish to save the results to. If no FileName parameter is specified, the output is sent to the console, and can be redirected from there.
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Specifies that Report output should be in HTML format. Use either this or the -ReportXML parameter, but not both.
Specifies that Report output should be in XML format. Use either this or the -ReportHTML parameter, but not both.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
This optional parameter specifies the file path you wish to save the results to. If no FileName parameter is specified, the output is sent to the console, and can be redirected from there.
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Specifies that Report output should be in HTML format. Use either this or the -ReportXML parameter, but not both.
Specifies that Report output should be in XML format. Use either this or the -ReportHTML parameter, but not both.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
This optional parameter specifies the file path you wish to save the results to. If no FileName parameter is specified, the output is sent to the console, and can be redirected from there.
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Specifies that Report output should be in HTML format. Use either this or the -ReportXML parameter, but not both.
Specifies that Report output should be in XML format. Use either this or the -ReportHTML parameter, but not both.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Reports the settings in the Default Domain Policy GPO to a file called DDPReport.HTML in the d:\data folder, in HTML Format

maml:description.maml:para: Outputs a RSOP Logging (GP Results) report for a given computer/user.

This cmdlet lets you generate an RSOP Logging (GP Results) report against computername and user name. You can also use this cmdlet to list the users on a remote system who actually have RSOP data (i.e. they have logged into the system at least once).
Specify the computer name you wish to connect to to collect RSOP data.

Optionally specify a user name on the remote computer for whom you wish to collect RSOP data. Note that the user can be specified as either <username> or <domain\username>. If you wish to see if a particular user has RSOP data on the remote system, then use the -ListUsers parameter instead.
This optional parameter specifies the file path you wish to save the results to. If no FileName parameter is specified, the output is sent to the console, and can be redirected from there.
Specifies that Report output should be in HTML format. Use either this or the -ReportXML parameter, but not both.
Specifies that Report output should be in XML format. Use either this or the -ReportHTML parameter, but not both.
Use this parameter if you wish to exclude per-computer RSOP data from the report. Note that you cannot use both this parameter and the -NoUser parameter together.
Use this parameter if you wish to exclude per-user RSOP data from the report. Note that you cannot use both this parameter and the -NoComputer parameter together.

Use this parameter instead of the -UserName parameter to simply list all the users on the specified computername that have RSOP data associated with them. You can use this parameter first to ensure that a particular does indeed have RSOP data on the target system.
Specify the computer name you wish to connect to to collect RSOP data.

Optionally specify a user name on the remote computer for whom you wish to collect RSOP data. Note that the user can be specified as either <username> or <domain\username>. If you wish to see if a particular user has RSOP data on the remote system, then use the -ListUsers parameter instead.
This optional parameter specifies the file path you wish to save the results to. If no FileName parameter is specified, the output is sent to the console, and can be redirected from there.
Specifies that Report output should be in HTML format. Use either this or the -ReportXML parameter, but not both.
Specifies that Report output should be in XML format. Use either this or the -ReportHTML parameter, but not both.
Use this parameter if you wish to exclude per-computer RSOP data from the report. Note that you cannot use both this parameter and the -NoUser parameter together.
Use this parameter if you wish to exclude per-user RSOP data from the report. Note that you cannot use both this parameter and the -NoComputer parameter together.

Use this parameter instead of the -UserName parameter to simply list all the users on the specified computername that have RSOP data associated with them. You can use this parameter first to ensure that a particular does indeed have RSOP data on the target system.

maml:alert.maml:para: Note that this cmdlet makes a remote connection to the workstation or server you specify with the ComputerName parameter. If you don't have remote DCOM access to that system, or those ports are firewalled, this command will fail.

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example retrieves RSOP data from the computer called "XP3" and user on that computer called "kmyer" and sends the results, in XML format, to a file called d:\data\xp3RSOP.XML
d:\data\xp3RSOp.XML
This example retrieves a list of user on the computer called XP3, that have RSOP data on that computer.

TrusteeSid : S-1-5-21-1993962763-1563985344-1708537768-1003 TrusteeName : TrusteeDomain : TrusteeDSPath : TrusteeType : 8
TrusteeSid : S-1-5-21-817735531-4269160403-1409475253-1107 TrusteeName : kmyer TrusteeDomain : CPANDL TrusteeDSPath : CN=Ken Myer,OU=Users,OU=Marketing,DC=cpandl,DC=com TrusteeType : 1
TrusteeSid : S-1-5-21-817735531-4269160403-1409475253-500 TrusteeName : Administrator TrusteeDomain : CPANDL TrusteeDSPath : CN=Administrator,CN=Users,DC=cpandl,DC=com TrusteeType : 1
TrusteeSid : S-1-5-21-1993962763-1563985344-1708537768-500 TrusteeName : TrusteeDomain : TrusteeDSPath : TrusteeType : 8
TrusteeSid : S-1-5-21-817735531-4269160403-1409475253-1605 TrusteeName : test TrusteeDomain : CPANDL TrusteeDSPath : CN=Test User,OU=PackageTest,DC=cpandl,DC=com TrusteeType : 1
TrusteeSid : S-1-5-21-817735531-4269160403-1409475253-1124 TrusteeName : bwestin TrusteeDomain : CPANDL TrusteeDSPath : CN=Bill Westin,OU=Users,OU=Marketing,DC=cpandl,DC=com TrusteeType : 1
TrusteeSid : S-1-5-21-817735531-4269160403-1409475253-3136 TrusteeName : jtibble TrusteeDomain : CPANDL TrusteeDSPath : CN=Jim Tibble,OU=Users,OU=Sales,DC=cpandl,DC=com TrusteeType : 1

maml:description.maml:para: Given a GPO name and scope, removes the link on the scope for that GPO
This cmdlet lets you removes a particular GPO link from the scope provided. The scope is an AD site, domain or OU.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the AD container path that you wish to un-link the GPO from (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the AD container path that you wish to un-link the GPO from (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example removes the Desktop Lockdown Policy GPO link from the Marketing OU. Note that this does not delete the GPO, only the link.
None

maml:description.maml:para: Deletes a GPO from a domain
Deletes the specified GPO from the specified AD domain
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

dev:remarks.maml:para: removes a GPO called "Engineering Desktop Lockdown" from the current domain. Note that when no -Domain parameter is specified, the current domain in which the cmdlet is running is assumed.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes a permission for a particular computer, user or group from a GPO

This cmdlet will remove a permission for a particular computer, user or group (trustee) from the chosen GPO. Note that you have to specify the trustee, the type of permission to remove and the GPO to remove it from.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to remove from a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Apply Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to process a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Edit Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to edit a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.

Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Modify permission that allows the trustee to edit, modify security and delete a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.

Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Read Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to read a GPO only (not apply). NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to remove from a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Apply Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to process a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Edit Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to edit a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.

Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Modify permission that allows the trustee to edit, modify security and delete a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.

Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Read Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to read a GPO only (not apply). NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to remove from a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Apply Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to process a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to remove from a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Edit Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to edit a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to remove from a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Modify permission that allows the trustee to edit, modify security and delete a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to remove from a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Read Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to read a GPO only (not apply). NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the computer, user or group name that you wish to remove from a GPO. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Apply Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to process a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Edit Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to edit a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.

Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Modify permission that allows the trustee to edit, modify security and delete a GPO. NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.

Specifies to remove the permission that grants the computer, user or group the Read Group Policy permission that allows the trustee to read a GPO only (not apply). NOTE: Choose only one of the Perm* options.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes the edit permission from the GP Editors group on the GPO called Folder Redirection Policy
remove-sdmgposecurity -Name "Folder Redirection Policy" -Trustee "cpandl\GP Editors" -PermEdit

Inherited : False Inheritable : True Denied : False Permission : 65536 Trustee : System.__ComObject
Inherited : False Inheritable : True Denied : False Permission : 65794 Trustee : System.__ComObject

maml:description.maml:para: Removes a GP-related permission from a given Scope of Management (SOM)

Use this cmdlet to remove a particular GP-related permission (e.g. Create GPO, Link GPO, RSoP logging or planning) from a given SOM (e.g. site, domain or OU). Note that you must specify the permission and trustee you wish to remove and that you can only remove one permission per command.
Provide the AD container path that you wish to remove GP related permissions on (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)
Provide the user or group name that has permissions you wish to remove from a SOM. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

Specifies the permission that revokes the user or group the Create GPO permission that allows the trustee to create a GPO in the domain. If you use this option, you must specify the domain DN on the Scope parameter NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

Specifies the permission that revokes the user or group the Link GPO permission that allows the trustee to Link a GPO to a site, domain or OU. NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

Specifies the permission that revokes the user or group the Generate RSOP Logging permission that allows the trustee to run RSOP Logging (Group Policy Results) against targets in the Scope specified. NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

Specifies the permission that revokes the user or group the Generate RSOP Planning permission that allows the trustee to run RSOP Planning (Group Policy Modeling) against targets in the Scope specified. NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.
Provide the AD container path that you wish to remove GP related permissions on (e.g. OU=Marketing, DC=cpandl, DC=com)
Provide the user or group name that has permissions you wish to remove from a SOM. Name must follow format of <domain\username>. For example: cpandl\darren
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.

Specifies the permission that revokes the user or group the Create GPO permission that allows the trustee to create a GPO in the domain. If you use this option, you must specify the domain DN on the Scope parameter NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

Specifies the permission that revokes the user or group the Link GPO permission that allows the trustee to Link a GPO to a site, domain or OU. NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

Specifies the permission that revokes the user or group the Generate RSOP Logging permission that allows the trustee to run RSOP Logging (Group Policy Results) against targets in the Scope specified. NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

Specifies the permission that revokes the user or group the Generate RSOP Planning permission that allows the trustee to run RSOP Planning (Group Policy Modeling) against targets in the Scope specified. NOTE: This version of the cmdlet only supports selecting one Perm* parameter per command.

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example removes the Link GPO permission for the GPO Admins group from the Marketing OU in the cpandl.com domain.

maml:description.maml:para: Let's you delete a Starter GPO by name
This cmdlet lets you delete an existing Starter GPO from a domain
Provide the friendly name of the Starter GPO you wish to delete.
Provide the DNS domain name of the starter GPO you wish to delete
Provide the friendly name of the Starter GPO you wish to delete.
Provide the DNS domain name of the starter GPO you wish to delete

maml:alert.maml:para: Note that this cmdlet works on Windows Server 2008, Vista, SP1 and above

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example will delete a Starter GPO called "Test IE Security Starter"

Confirm Are you sure you want to perform this action? Performing operation "Deleting Starter GPO" on Target "Test IE Security Starter". [Y] Yes [A] Yes to All [N] No [L] No to All [S] Suspend [?] Help (default is "Y"): y Deletion Successful

maml:description.maml:para: Removes a WMI Filter link from a specified GPO
This cmdlet will remove any WMI filter linked to a GPO. Since a GPO can only have 1 WMI filter link, any link present will be removed.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes any WMI Filter linked to the GPO called "Test GPO"
GPO Test GPO cleared of WMI Filter

maml:description.maml:para: Restore a GPO from backup

Restores a GPO from a backup. requires that you have the Backup ID of the GPO you wish to restore. The backup ID is different from the GPO GUID. If the GPO exists, it is overwritten by the restore. If the GPO does not exist, it is restored from scratch. Note that GPO Links are not restored.
This is the ID of the Backup you wish to restore. It takes the form of a GUID string.
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
The location where the backup files reside. Note that this can be a literal path (e.g. c:\gpbackups) or a UNC path (\\server1\gpbackups).
This is the ID of the Backup you wish to restore. It takes the form of a GUID string.
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
The location where the backup files reside. Note that this can be a literal path (e.g. c:\gpbackups) or a UNC path (\\server1\gpbackups).

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example restores a GPO backup with the ID shown in the GUID string from the path on SDM1

maml:description.maml:para: Sets or changes the owner of a GPO

This cmdlet can be used to change the owner user or group of a GPO. Essentially you provide the new owner name in the form of domain\user or group (e.g. cpandl\darren) and the GPO's ownership is changed
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the user or group name you wish to assign ownership of the selected GPO. Use the form <domain\user or group>. For example, cpandl\darren or cpandl\domain admins
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"
Provide the user or group name you wish to assign ownership of the selected GPO. Use the form <domain\user or group>. For example, cpandl\darren or cpandl\domain admins
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GPO's display name (i.e. friendly name).
Provide the user or group name you wish to assign ownership of the selected GPO. Use the form <domain\user or group>. For example, cpandl\darren or cpandl\domain admins
Provide the DNS domain name of the GPO you wish to manage.
Provide the GUID of the GPO, in the format of "{5A7BDD33-A51C-4C53-952E-22CC3E3A76FF}"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Changes the owner on the "My Lockdown Policy" GPO to the group called "Cpandl\GPO Admins"
Changes the owner on the GPO called "Security GPO" to the user account "Cpandl\darren"

maml:description.maml:para: Triggers a GP refresh (GPUpdate) on a remote system.

This cmdlet uses WMI to trigger a GPUpdate on a remote system. You can pass any of the usual GPUpdate parameters to the cmdlet and you can selectively refresh computer or user-side policy (default is both).
The computer name (host name) of the machine you wish to trigger a remote GP refresh on.
(optional) The user name you wish to use to connect to the remote computer specified. If not specified, then the current user context is used.
(optional) The password you wish to use to connect to the remote computer specified. Expects a securestring value like that generated by Get-Credential.
Use this parameter to only trigger a computer-side refresh
Use this parameter to only trigger a user-side refresh
Use this parameter to issue a -force along with the gpupdate command.
Use this parameter to issue a -logoff along with the gpupdate command.
Use this parameter to issue a -boot along with the gpupdate command.
Use this parameter to issue a -sync along with the gpupdate command.
If specified, and the gpupdate command asks for a reboot or re-logon (often when -force is used), then that prompt will be ignored--i.e. the machine will not reboot or re-logon.
The computer name (host name) of the machine you wish to trigger a remote GP refresh on.
(optional) The user name you wish to use to connect to the remote computer specified. If not specified, then the current user context is used.
(optional) The password you wish to use to connect to the remote computer specified. Expects a securestring value like that generated by Get-Credential.
Use this parameter to only trigger a computer-side refresh
Use this parameter to only trigger a user-side refresh
Use this parameter to issue a -force along with the gpupdate command.
Use this parameter to issue a -logoff along with the gpupdate command.
Use this parameter to issue a -boot along with the gpupdate command.
Use this parameter to issue a -sync along with the gpupdate command.
If specified, and the gpupdate command asks for a reboot or re-logon (often when -force is used), then that prompt will be ignored--i.e. the machine will not reboot or re-logon.

command:example.maml:title: EXAMPLE 1

maml:introduction.maml:para: Force a user-side GP refresh on a machine called Win10Client

command:example.dev:code:
C:\PS> Update-SDMGp -ComputerName win10client -User -Force
Group Policy refresh delivered successfully on machine: win10client
